title: calystegia subacaulis subsp. episcopalis
intro: Calystegia subacaulis subsp. episcopalis, also known as Bishop's morning glory, is a flowering plant that belongs to the Convolvulaceae family. It is used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. The plant is said to have health benefits such as reducing pain and inflammation, as well as acting as a natural sedative, and is often used to treat anxiety and insomnia. Calystegia subacaulis subsp. episcopalis can be prepared as a tea or tincture for its medicinal uses, and is also used in some herbal remedies to promote relaxation and improve sleep quality.
